skill.name: project-operator
skill.description: Guides safe operation and debugging of a local API, worker, and frontend stack using logs, status checks, and clear change planning.
skill.summary: Operate and debug a local application stack with observable, reversible changes.
skill.author: ACSKamloops

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged a weak cryptographic algorithm at SKILL.md:3, but the line is a plain-language description and contains no cryptographic API or algorithm. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, command execution, network access, or secret access evidence was found in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: The static finding points to the word "stack" in the description. This is operational wording for an API, worker, and frontend stack, not a cryptographic algorithm or implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 contains only metadata text describing local stack operation. There is no cryptographic code, algorithm name, or executable instruction on that line.

content.value_statement: Local application stacks can be hard to debug when restarts and config changes happen without context. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code toward logs, status checks, clear impact notes, and reversible operations.
actual_capabilities: Guides debugging across a local API, worker, and frontend stack.
Prioritizes logs and status endpoints before process restarts.
Encourages incremental changes that preserve uptime and clarity.
Asks for plain-language impact notes when changing configuration.
Promotes revert paths for configuration changes.
Requests stop or cancel strategies for long-running commands.
limitations: Provides operating guidance only; it does not include scripts or tools.
Does not define specific commands for any framework or process manager.
Does not replace human review for production-impacting operations.
Does not add monitoring, alerting, or deployment integrations by itself.

use_cases.title: Triage a failing local service
use_cases.description: Use logs and status endpoints to understand which part of the stack is failing before restarting processes.

use_cases.title: Plan a configuration change
use_cases.description: Describe expected impact, apply a small change, and keep a clear revert path for quick recovery.

use_cases.title: Run long local tasks safely
use_cases.description: Start a long-running command with clear status updates and a documented stop or cancel strategy.

prompt_templates.title: Check stack health
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner triage of a local project stack.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the local API, worker, and frontend status. Start with logs and status endpoints before recommending any restart.

prompt_templates.title: Debug a failing component
prompt_templates.scenario: Focused investigation after one service appears unhealthy.
prompt_templates.prompt: Investigate why the local worker is failing. Gather observable evidence first, explain likely causes, and propose the smallest safe next step.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a config update
prompt_templates.scenario: Changing local configuration with recovery planning.
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan this configuration change. Explain the impact in plain language, identify validation steps, and include a revert path before editing files.

prompt_templates.title: Operate a long task
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow for commands that may keep running.
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the required long-running local command. Provide progress updates, explain how to stop it safely, and avoid restarting services unless evidence supports it.

output_examples.input: The frontend is not loading, and the API may be down.
output_examples.output: The assistant checks frontend and API signals, reviews recent logs, reports the observed failure point, and recommends a targeted next action.

output_examples.input: Change the worker configuration for local testing.
output_examples.output: The assistant explains the expected impact, lists validation checks, applies the smallest relevant change, and documents how to revert it.

output_examples.input: Start the local stack and watch for failures.
output_examples.output: The assistant starts the needed process, tracks logs, gives status updates, and states how to stop the running command.

best_practices: Check logs and status endpoints before restarting any process.
Keep configuration changes small, observable, and easy to reverse.
State stop or cancel steps before starting long-running commands.
anti_patterns: Restarting services before collecting evidence from logs or health checks.
Changing configuration without explaining impact or rollback steps.
Leaving long-running commands active without a clear stop strategy.

faq.question: What does this skill help operate?
faq.answer: It helps operate and debug a local stack that includes API, worker, and frontend components.

faq.question: Does it include executable scripts?
faq.answer: No. The skill contains operating guidance, not bundled scripts, commands, or automation files.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed file contains guidance text and no confirmed dangerous behavior.

faq.question: Why was a cryptography issue reported?
faq.answer: The static scanner flagged line 3, but that line only describes a local application stack.

faq.question: Can it restart services automatically?
faq.answer: No. It advises checking logs and status endpoints before restarts, but defines no automatic restart mechanism.

faq.question: Who should use this skill?
faq.answer: Developers and operators who need disciplined local debugging, observable changes, and clear recovery steps.
